body: We normally use PascalCase values for internal enums.
Rename to targetDeposit, might be something else than a staking route (e.g. LM-Route in future).
Index should be on asset, targetDeposit and user. But will probably not work, because the user should be able to create same route for different chains.
Index should be ok without amount, shouldn't it?
Not related to this feature, but I would set outTxId `nullable: true` and change all places where we filter for `outTxId: ''` with `outTxId: null`.
Hm. It is actually possible to send two inputs with the same asset in a single TX to the same address 🤔  I think we need to add the vout to the table. Index will then be on TX vout, asset and route.
Sorry, forgot the comma: TX, vout, asset and route.
Wir haben die Variablen normalerweise mit `Sepa` und nicht `SEPA` benannt, wäre jetzt ein Bruch 🤔
Perhaps write a util method to create a string of predefined length filled with a given character? Would make it more readable 😉

```ts
const fiat = {...new Fiat()};
fiat instanceof Fiat // false
```
Perhaps better use `Object.assign(new Fiat(), { ...defaultFiat, ...customValues })` (for all mocks)?
Rename to identIdentificationId**s** to be clear?
Use the same logic as for country mock here (add a default UserData and use the Object.assign())
Should we switch to object destructuring (for all mocks)?

Could this be replace by the following (not sure)?
```ts
return this.expiration < new Date();
```
Why do you take a substring here? Column length is 'MAX'.
What about an enum for the bank name?
This method does exactly the same as `confirmSentMail` 🤔
Do we send `buyCryptoCrypto` notifications, even when AML check is failed?
Should we return null, or rather throw an error? Users might get weird emails, if we return null here.

Simpler
```ts
return this.users.find((e) => e.wallet.isKycClient === true) != null;
```
or
```ts
return !!this.users.find((e) => e.wallet.isKycClient === true);
```
Should we move this ratio somewhere more central?
This should probably be nullable: true, since it's set only after creation?
I would move this to personal-mail and try to use it in the body. Would this be possible?
Hehe 😄  I meant the method could be renamed to mailSent. The current name is a bit strange 😉

```ts
      templateParams: {
        ...defaultParams,
        salutation: 'Hi DFX Tech Support',
        body: ErrorMonitoringMail.createBody(params.errors),
      },
```
or just
```ts
      templateParams: {
        date: new Date().getFullYear(),
        salutation: 'Hi DFX Tech Support',
        body: ErrorMonitoringMail.createBody(params.errors),
      },
```
I think we can remove this.
I think we can remove this
Maybe `type` is a better naming than `reason`, WDYT? Sorry, `reason` was my proposition 😇
Reason should be required IMO (also in DTO)
This logic is kind of hidden (status change). Maybe we should name the method differently?
Output reference is set after batching?
I would invert it to isDfxUser, makes more sense IMO.

Remove the index name, is not really helpful
`@Index((user: User) => [user.address, user.blockchain], { unique: true })`
Should not be nullable. Maybe add a TODO to change this later?
Can it be null, if TX is complete?
Can it still be null after payout is complete?

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/85513542/208694834-cd777c1c-62e0-40de-99b1-ff99493f0721.png)
Ok, maybe it would make sense to not call this method, if fee is null? Do the check in BuyCrypto?
Logic for confirmation was different before.
Maybe rename it to SignInLog or similar?
Is it ok to have two pipelines for the same rule, one in CREATED and the other in IN_PROGRESS state?

Are there any other interesting statuses?
- Pricing Mismatch
- Missing Liquidity
